paper_title: Analysis of Radiolarians in the Japan Sea during the Late Miocene

methods.description: This method estimates the absolute abundances of total radiolarians and C. nakasekoi in terms of the number of skeletons per gram of dry sediment collected at IODP Site U1425. It involves sample preparation and counting radiolarians under a microscope.

steps.description: The samples were freeze-dried and then treated with diluted hydrogen peroxide (10%) and hydrochloric acid (5%) to remove organic and calcareous matter.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare samples by freeze-drying and treating with hydrogen peroxide and hydrochloric acid

steps.description: The undissolved residue in each sample was sieved through a 45 µm screen.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve undissolved residue through a 45 µm screen

steps.description: Once the undissolved residue was washed, we mounted the residue on Q-slides for quantitative radiolarian studies.

To prepare Q-slides, the undissolved residue was transferred to a 100-mL beaker containing 100 mL of water. The solution was then mixed, and a 0.2 mL sample was taken from the suspension using a micropipette and dropped onto a cover glass of area 22 × 18 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer residue to beaker, mix, and sample with micropipette onto cover glass

steps.description: Count all the radiolarians in a Q-slide under an optical microscope at magnifications of 100 × to 400 ×.
Estimate the total radiolarian absolute abundances in 1 g of dry sediment using the provided equation.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate radiolarian absolute abundances using equation

methods.description: This method estimates the accumulation rates of total radiolarians and selected species to monitor water mass changes in the Japan Sea during the Late Miocene.

steps.description: Estimate the total radiolarian accumulation rates using the linear sedimentation rate and GRA bulk density.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate radiolarian accumulation rates using sedimentation rate and bulk density

steps.description: Estimate the accumulation rates of each species using the decantation method and relative abundances.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate species accumulation rates using decantation method and relative abundances
